source: Municipalities of Singapore, Penang and Malacca accounts__CO273:673:5
filename: Municipalities of Singapore, Penang and Malacca accounts__CO273:673:5.txt
summary:
- File concerns **accounts and audit of the “available funds”** of the **Singapore, Penang, and Malacca Municipalities** (and, for Singapore, the **Singapore Improvement Trust (S.I.T.)**), including correspondence about how the accounts should be prepared, audited, and evidenced.  
- **Geography/time period:** Malaya-related municipalities (**Singapore, Penang, Malacca**) with transactions handled in **London** and payments made via **Australia (Sydney)** and **India (incl. Bangalore)**; main accounting years covered are **1942–1944**, with audit/administrative follow-up in **1943–1945**.  
- **Key actors/organizations:** **Director of Colonial Audit** and auditors; **Colonial Office**; **Crown Agents** (incl. correspondence with **S. Bullwinkle** and **J. Goldburg**); municipal agents **Messrs. Peirce & Williams** (compile final accounts); references to **Malayan Planning Unit** and **Loans Committee**; letters signed by **H. Palmer** and minutes by **K. Reed**.  
- **Major issues/decisions:** repeated audit concern that **payments made in Australia** (Singapore & Penang) were included in statements **without vouchers available**; in June 1945 Palmer asks Bullwinkle to arrange for vouchers in Australia to be **sent to the UK** via Peirce & Williams.  
- **Accounting presentation change proposed/accepted:** request that **S.I.T. transactions** previously mixed into Singapore municipal accounts be **separately stated** going forward, and that a statement be prepared for S.I.T. transactions **up to 31 Dec 1943**; Crown Agents indicate agreement with Peirce & Williams’ proposal to incorporate past accounts from **1 Jan 1942** in later copies (timing described as “end of the current year,” exact year unclear in OCR).  
- **Notable audit observations:** allowances to **dependants** were paid under **Colonial Office authority**; payments to pensioners/officials/dependants often supported only by **bank statements**, with **acknowledgments/receipts** being sought; **ex-gratia interest** paid on **Municipal Dollar Loans** under Loans Committee authority; **interest due on dollar securities** not received and **no claims** made; **no contributions to Sinking Funds**, though interest continued to accumulate.  
- **Notable numbers (examples):** Singapore audit notes unsettled diverted/undelivered stores totaling **£9,683.9.3** (including **£9,348.6.8** “taken over by Australia” with no settlement claim yet); Penang analogous unsettled stores total **£1,011.18.0**. Opening/closing balances and receipts/payments are detailed for each municipality (e.g., Singapore opening total funds **£3,348,257.7.11** at **1 Jan 1942**; Malacca municipal fund securities **£5,800** nominal at **1/1/42**).
